forecast-period.text: A trough will linger inland over the west until a cold front sweeps across southern SA on Thursday. This front will push the inland trough into the far north, exiting the state early Friday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate in the wake of the front as a strong high pressure system moves in from the west from Friday, establishing in the Bight on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northwesterly during the afternoon and evening.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly 20 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the late evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly in the late afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres inshore.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the evening.
Cloudy.
